rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use visible identifiers from the current case when writing AGREE expressions
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component type features
Place assignments in component implementation annexes when they reference subcomponent features
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the scope of the intended target component or implementation
Preserve existing AADL architecture by not modifying component types or implementations beyond annex insertion
Use only visible identifiers from the current case when writing AGREE expressions
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component type features
Place assignments in component implementation annexes when they reference subcomponent features
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for contracts
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for equations and `const name : type = value;` for constants
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not place assumptions and guarantees in implementation annexes when they reference component type features
